metadata.refrigerant_note: R-410A is the dominant refrigerant 2010–2025. R-22 found on pre-2010 units. R-32/R-454B appearing on 2025+ units.
metadata.pricing_note: Houston retail pricing (customer-facing, parts + labor). Wholesale parts cost is 40–60% of retail part price shown.
metadata.pressure_note: Pressures given for 208/230V single-phase residential split systems. Houston summer peak: 95–100°F OAT typical.
gaps_resolved: GAP-01: R-410A heat pump heating mode pressure tables added
GAP-02: R-32 full pressure table (75-105°F) added
GAP-03: R-454B complete section added (pressures, superheat, subcooling, systems)
GAP-04: Variable speed inverter amp specs by tonnage added
GAP-05: Piston bore reference table (R-410A and R-22) added
GAP-06: 1.5T compressor added to parts catalog
GAP-07: Heat pump heating mode superheat/subcooling targets added
GAP-08: Gas furnace section added (manifold pressure, temp rise, flame sensor, inducer, CO)
GAP-09: Dual fuel capability flags added to brand series + detection logic
GAP-10: LG, Samsung, Gree, Pioneer, MrCool, Bosch added to error_code_db
GAP-11: Condenser fan motor FLA added to all tonnage specs
GAP-12: Fixed orifice superheat lower bound corrected to 8°F + CTOA chart added
GAP-13: Pre-2010 legacy model prefixes added for all major brands
GAP-14: Refrigerant per foot corrected by liquid line pipe size per tonnage
GAP-15: Heat pump defrost cycle data added
GAP-16: ECM speed tap guide and CFM targets added
GAP-17: R-22 Houston market pricing note added
GAP-18: 3-pole contactor added to parts catalog
GAP-19: TXV hunting diagnostic branch added to fault_pressure_signatures

brands.name: Carrier
brands.houston_notes: Top 1-2 brand in Houston. Strong dealer network. Parts widely stocked at Johnstone Supply, Carrier Enterprise.

series.name: Performance
series.dual_fuel_note: Carrier Performance outdoor can be dual-fuel with compatible gas furnace + Hybrid Heat thermostat.

series.name: Infinity
series.dual_fuel_note: Carrier Infinity 24VNA outdoor pairs with 59MN/59TP gas furnace for dual-fuel. Infinity system board manages switchover setpoint.

brands.legacy_notes: Carrier pre-2010 model numbers start with 38 (outdoor) or FA/FE (air handler). 38TXA = XL series, 38TDB = Comfort series. Many still running in Houston.

brands.name: Trane
brands.houston_notes: Premium brand. Large commercial and residential presence in Houston. Known for durability in high-humidity climates.

series.name: XR
series.dual_fuel_note: Trane XR series can be configured dual-fuel with compatible furnace and Nexia thermostat.

series.name: XV
series.dual_fuel_note: Trane XV20i/XV21i pairs with XC80/S9V2 gas furnace for dual-fuel (ComfortLink II).

brands.legacy_notes: Trane pre-2010: XB = base, XR = mid. 4-digit prefix + tonnage code + efficiency code. Look for R-22 refrigerant label.

brands.name: Lennox
brands.houston_notes: Headquartered in Richardson, TX. Strong Texas presence. Signature series is highest SEER2 in market (28 SEER2).

series.name: Signature / XC
series.dual_fuel_note: Lennox XP series + Dave Lennox Signature SL280 furnace = dual-fuel. iComfort S30 thermostat manages switchover.

brands.legacy_notes: Lennox pre-2010: XC = Merit/Elite AC, XP = Elite heat pump, HS = Signature. 13-14 SEER era.

brands.name: Goodman
brands.houston_notes: Manufactured in Houston, TX (Daikin Texas Technology Park, Waller, TX). Most affordable name brand. Parts always available locally. Dominant in new construction and replacement.

series.name: GSXV
series.dual_fuel_note: Goodman GSXV / Amana AVXC20 dual fuel with GMVC96 or AMVC96 furnace + Ecobee/Honeywell hybrid thermostat.

brands.legacy_notes: Goodman pre-2010: GSX = gold series AC, SSX = silver series, GSH/GSC = heat pump. Very common in Houston rental properties.

brands.name: Rheem
brands.houston_notes: Strong presence in Texas. Prestige series rated 22.5 SEER2. Ruud is identical internally.

series.name: Prestige
series.dual_fuel_note: Rheem Prestige RP20 + RGRM 96% furnace dual-fuel via EcoNet smart thermostat.

brands.legacy_notes: Rheem/Ruud pre-2010: RA/UA = air conditioner, RP/UP = heat pump. RAKA common entry-level. Very common in Houston builder homes.

brands.name: York
brands.houston_notes: Solid mid-market brand. Often found in commercial-adjacent residential. Parts available at Johnson Controls distributors.
brands.legacy_notes: York pre-2010 model prefix is less standardized. H1RA/H2RA common on residential split. ZH = Stellar series.

brands.name: Daikin
brands.houston_notes: World's largest HVAC manufacturer. Daikin Texas Technology Park in Waller, TX — largest HVAC factory in North America. Growing residential market share rapidly.

series.name: Daikin Fit
series.notes: Slim side-discharge cabinet. Quiet (56 dBA). Popular in Houston for tight installations.

brands.name: Bryant
brands.houston_notes: Identical to Carrier internally. Often slightly lower price. Same parts, same specs as Carrier equivalent series.
brands.legacy_notes: Bryant is Carrier sister brand. Legacy Bryant uses 3-digit model numbers unlike modern 24xxx format.

brands.name: American Standard
brands.houston_notes: Identical to Trane internally. Same compressors, coils, specs. Separate dealer network.

brands.name: Amana
brands.houston_notes: Budget premium brand (lifetime compressor warranty). Identical to Goodman internally. Same parts.

brands.name: Ruud
brands.houston_notes: Identical to Rheem internally. Often installed by different contractor networks. Same parts, same specs.
brands.legacy_notes: Rheem/Ruud pre-2010: RA/UA = air conditioner, RP/UP = heat pump. RAKA common entry-level. Very common in Houston builder homes.

brands.name: Heil
brands.houston_notes: Common in Houston older installs. Same internals as York. ICP distributor network.

brands.name: Coleman
brands.houston_notes: Budget end of Johnson Controls family. Same specs as York LX/Affinity.

brands.name: Payne
brands.houston_notes: Budget Carrier brand. Identical specs to Carrier Comfort. Often installed in new construction.

brands.name: Mitsubishi Electric
brands.houston_notes: Primarily mini-splits and multi-zone ducted. Growing in Houston additions/sunrooms. Different diagnostic approach than central AC.

series.name: M-Series / MSZ
series.notes: Mini-split/multi-zone. Lineset length affects charge. Very different from central AC diagnostics.

series.name: Hyper-Heat / H2i
series.notes: Heats down to -13F. Rarely needed in Houston but premium option.

electrical_specs_by_tonnage.notes: Values are typical ranges for R-410A single-phase 208/230V residential split systems. Always verify against actual nameplate. Variable-speed (inverter) systems have much lower steady-state RLA. IMPORTANT: Variable-speed (inverter) systems have much lower steady-state amp draw than these tables. See variable_speed_inverter_specs for correct thresholds. The 10% over RLA rule does NOT apply to inverter compressors.

condenser_fan_motor_fla.overamp_rule: If condenser fan motor draws >1.5A = over-amping (bearing drag, capacitor weak, or winding fault)
condenser_fan_motor_fla.fault_card: condenser_fan_motor_failure (see diagnostic path — NOT Card #4 which is indoor blower)

specs.lineset_note: Liquid line 3/8" → 0.5 oz/ft charge add per foot beyond factory-standard 15ft lineset. Previous value of 0.6 oz/ft was wrong for 1.5T and 4-5T systems.

condenser_fan_motor_fla.overamp_rule: If condenser fan motor draws >1.7A = over-amping (bearing drag, capacitor weak, or winding fault)
condenser_fan_motor_fla.fault_card: condenser_fan_motor_failure (see diagnostic path — NOT Card #4 which is indoor blower)

specs.lineset_note: Liquid line 3/8" or 1/2" → 0.55 oz/ft charge add per foot beyond factory-standard 15ft lineset. Previous value of 0.6 oz/ft was wrong for 1.5T and 4-5T systems.

condenser_fan_motor_fla.overamp_rule: If condenser fan motor draws >1.8A = over-amping (bearing drag, capacitor weak, or winding fault)
condenser_fan_motor_fla.fault_card: condenser_fan_motor_failure (see diagnostic path — NOT Card #4 which is indoor blower)

specs.lineset_note: Liquid line 1/2" → 0.6 oz/ft charge add per foot beyond factory-standard 15ft lineset. Previous value of 0.6 oz/ft was wrong for 1.5T and 4-5T systems.

condenser_fan_motor_fla.overamp_rule: If condenser fan motor draws >2.0A = over-amping (bearing drag, capacitor weak, or winding fault)
condenser_fan_motor_fla.fault_card: condenser_fan_motor_failure (see diagnostic path — NOT Card #4 which is indoor blower)

specs.lineset_note: Liquid line 1/2" → 0.6 oz/ft charge add per foot beyond factory-standard 15ft lineset. Previous value of 0.6 oz/ft was wrong for 1.5T and 4-5T systems.

condenser_fan_motor_fla.overamp_rule: If condenser fan motor draws >2.2A = over-amping (bearing drag, capacitor weak, or winding fault)
condenser_fan_motor_fla.fault_card: condenser_fan_motor_failure (see diagnostic path — NOT Card #4 which is indoor blower)

specs.lineset_note: Liquid line 1/2" → 0.6 oz/ft charge add per foot beyond factory-standard 15ft lineset. Previous value of 0.6 oz/ft was wrong for 1.5T and 4-5T systems.

condenser_fan_motor_fla.overamp_rule: If condenser fan motor draws >2.4A = over-amping (bearing drag, capacitor weak, or winding fault)
condenser_fan_motor_fla.fault_card: condenser_fan_motor_failure (see diagnostic path — NOT Card #4 which is indoor blower)

specs.lineset_note: Liquid line 5/8" → 0.8 oz/ft charge add per foot beyond factory-standard 15ft lineset. Previous value of 0.6 oz/ft was wrong for 1.5T and 4-5T systems.

condenser_fan_motor_fla.overamp_rule: If condenser fan motor draws >2.9A = over-amping (bearing drag, capacitor weak, or winding fault)
condenser_fan_motor_fla.fault_card: condenser_fan_motor_failure (see diagnostic path — NOT Card #4 which is indoor blower)

specs.lineset_note: Liquid line 5/8" → 0.8 oz/ft charge add per foot beyond factory-standard 15ft lineset. Previous value of 0.6 oz/ft was wrong for 1.5T and 4-5T systems.

variable_speed_inverter_specs.diagnostic_rule: For inverter/variable-speed compressors: do NOT compare to the standard RLA table. Compare measured amps to the NAMEPLATE VFD input amps. At partial load (30-60% capacity), amp draw is 30-60% of nameplate.
variable_speed_inverter_specs.overamp_rule: INVERTER RULE: Measure VFD input amps (at disconnect or load side). If >10% above NAMEPLATE VFD amps at full demand = investigate. If at partial load and reading nameplate or above = compressor struggling.
variable_speed_inverter_specs.lra_note: Inverter compressors have near-zero LRA (locked rotor amps) — the VFD ramps up current gradually. A standard hard-start kit is NOT needed and NOT compatible.

houston_climate_context.delta_t_note: Houston high humidity reduces sensible delta-T. 16–18°F is normal on a properly charged system. Values below 14°F or above 22°F warrant investigation.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.houston_note: Typical Houston summer peak day. Most calls occur at this range.
Common Houston peak. High discharge = check condenser coil cleanliness first.

fixed_orifice_piston_houston.note: CORRECTED: Houston minimum is 8°F, not 5°F. At 95°F OAT / 78°F WBT (typical Houston peak), target is 10-14°F. Below 8°F risks liquid refrigerant reaching compressor on a hot humid day.

ctoa_guidance.description: For precision charging, use CTOA method: target superheat varies with OAT and indoor wet bulb temperature.

txv_at_indoor_coil.note: TXV maintains its own superheat. 8–14°F normal. Use subcooling method at condenser for TXV charging, not superheat.

superheat_targets.heat_pump_heating_mode_note: WARNING: 5-12°F superheat targets above are for COOLING MODE ONLY. In heating mode, suction superheat target is 15-25°F. Applying cooling targets to a heat pump in heat mode will cause dangerous overcharge.

txv_systems.note: Measure at liquid line near condenser outlet. 10–15°F is the charging target for TXV systems. High subcooling = overcharge.

fixed_orifice_systems.note: Do NOT use subcooling for piston/fixed orifice systems. Use superheat method.

variable_speed_eev_systems.note: EEV modulates superheat internally. Use manufacturer spec. Subcooling 8–12°F typical.

houston_concern_low.note: Below 14°F: possible dirty filter, low charge, frozen coil, or high humidity (latent overload)

houston_concern_high.note: Above 22°F: possible restricted airflow or very dry indoor conditions (rare in Houston)

dirty_condenser_coil.note: Similar to overcharge — use coil photo to differentiate

fixed_orifice_undersized.note: Common in heat-mode low suction for heat pumps

txv_hunting.suction: OSCILLATING — swings ±5-10 psig over 30-60 second cycles
txv_hunting.discharge: OSCILLATING — minor swings ±10-15 psig in sync with suction
txv_hunting.superheat: OSCILLATING — swings ±5-10°F; average may be normal (8-14°F)
txv_hunting.how_to_distinguish_from_charge_problem: Watch suction pressure for 2-3 minutes. Charge problem = steady readings, low suction. TXV hunting = oscillating readings, average suction normal.
common_causes: TXV bulb loose or not making contact with suction line
Overcharge — too much liquid flooding back to TXV
TXV superheat setpoint too low (rare, factory-set)
Houston high-humidity causes rapid wet-bulb swings that destabilize TXV control
txv_hunting.resolution: Check TXV bulb clamp, insulation, and orientation. Verify charge via subcooling. If charge is correct and bulb is secure, TXV may need replacement.
txv_hunting.houston_note: TXV hunting is significantly more common in Houston due to extreme humidity (dew points 70°F+ in summer). Do NOT re-charge a system that is hunting — it will not fix the problem.

heat_pump_heating_mode.mode_note: In heating mode, outdoor coil = evaporator, indoor coil = condenser. Suction and discharge roles are REVERSED vs cooling. Never apply cooling mode pressure targets to a heat pump in heating mode.
heat_pump_heating_mode.charging_method: subcooling at liquid line (indoor coil outlet). Target 10-18°F subcooling in heating mode.

suction_superheat_heating.note: Target 15-25°F suction superheat in heating mode (outdoor coil evaporating). Higher than cooling mode. Below 12°F = possible overcharge in heat mode.

subcooling_heating.note: Indoor coil is now the condenser. Measure subcooling at liquid line service port near indoor unit.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.note: Near-freeze operation. Most systems will cycle into defrost frequently at this OAT.
Defrost initiation threshold for most systems. Expect periodic defrost cycles.
Normal mild-winter heating. Common Houston cold snap temperature.
Most common Houston heating-season temperature range.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.houston_note: Typical Houston winter day. Heat pump runs efficiently.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.note: Mild demand. Heat pump at high efficiency.
Low heating demand. Suction approaches cooling mode range as OAT rises.

diagnostic_flags.suction_too_low: Suction >15 psig below table min → undercharge OR restriction on suction line OR outdoor coil iced over
diagnostic_flags.suction_too_high: Suction >10 psig above table max → overcharge OR reversing valve not fully shifted to heating position
diagnostic_flags.discharge_too_low: Discharge <200 psig at any heating OAT → undercharge OR compressor weak
diagnostic_flags.discharge_too_high: Discharge >50 psig above table max → indoor coil dirty/restricted OR TXV/EEV stuck closed
diagnostic_flags.reversing_valve_check: If suction and discharge both in cooling-mode range while thermostat calls for heat → reversing valve stuck or solenoid failed

r22.notes: R-22 (Freon/HCFC-22). Found in pre-2010 units. Production banned in US since 2020. Reclaimed only. Very expensive — $250–400/lb retail installed.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.houston_note: Most Houston R-22 service calls at this OAT.

r22.houston_pricing_note: Houston R-22 pricing is LOWER than national average due to high volume of pre-2010 units still in service and competitive wholesale market (multiple Johnstone Supply, Wesco, and independent refrigerant wholesalers). Typical Houston R-22 installed price: $200-350/lb. National average: $250-400/lb. Never quote national pricing in Houston without checking current local wholesale rates first.

r32.notes: R-32 appearing in 2025+ units (Lennox Signature, Daikin, some Carrier). Higher operating pressures than R-410A. Mildly flammable (A2L class). Requires certified technician.
r32.pressure_note: R-32 operates at approximately 15–20% higher pressures than R-410A at same temperatures.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.note: R-32 runs ~15-20% higher pressure than R-410A at same conditions.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.houston_note: Typical Houston summer peak. R-32 high-side pressure is significantly higher than R-410A — do not confuse with overcharge.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.note: Extreme heat day. R-32 discharge can exceed 600 psig on a restricted system — high-pressure cutout risk.

txv_eev.note: Same range as R-410A for TXV/EEV systems.

fixed_orifice.note: R-32 units rarely ship with piston metering — if encountered, use same CTOA method as R-410A.

r32.heat_pump_heating_mode_note: R-32 heat pump heating mode: suction ~20-30% higher than R-410A at same OAT. Apply same logic as R-410A heat pump heating but expect suction 20-30 psig higher. Always consult manufacturer spec sheet for R-32 system charging targets.

r454b.notes: R-454B (Opteon XL41 / Puron Advance / Solstice 454B). Blend of 68.9% R-32 + 31.1% R-1234yf. EPA AIM Act mandated for new residential HVAC from Jan 1 2025. Mildly flammable (A2L). 5-12% more efficient than R-410A. GWP 466 (vs R-410A 2088).
r454b.safety_note: A2L refrigerant — certified technician required. Use A2L-rated recovery equipment. No open flames near equipment.
r454b.glide_note: R-454B is zeotropic (has temperature glide). Use DEW point pressure for suction-side diagnosis; BUBBLE point for liquid-line measurements.

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.note: R-454B discharge runs 5-10% lower than R-410A at same conditions.

bubble_dew_split.note: At 90°F condensing temp — use for high-side diagnosis

pressure_by_outdoor_ambient.houston_note: Typical Houston summer peak. R-454B runs slightly lower discharge than R-410A — do not interpret as low charge.

txv_eev_cooling.note: Similar to R-410A. Most 2025+ units use EEV — superheat is controlled by the unit, not set by tech.

fixed_orifice_cooling.note: Very rare on R-454B systems. If encountered, use subcooling method.

txv_eev_cooling.note: Primary charging method for R-454B. Measure at bubble point (liquid line).

heat_pump_heating_mode.note: Same heating mode logic as R-410A. Suction saturation 20-25°F below OAT. Target subcooling 10-18°F. Do NOT use cooling mode targets.

heat_pump_defrost_cycle.description: Heat pumps defrost the outdoor coil when ice accumulates during heating mode. Understanding defrost distinguishes normal operation from a fault.

initiation_conditions.demand_defrost_logic: Board compares outdoor ambient temp to outdoor coil temp. When differential exceeds 15-20°F AND coil temp drops below 28°F, defrost initiates. Typical interval: every 30-90 minutes in sustained cold.
initiation_conditions.timed_defrost_legacy: Older systems: fixed 30, 60, or 90 minute timer. Newer systems use demand logic.

defrost_operation.reversing_valve: Shifts to cooling mode (outdoor coil = condenser, indoor coil = evaporator)
defrost_operation.outdoor_fan: STOPS during defrost — this is normal
defrost_operation.indoor_fan: May run or stop depending on manufacturer; some run auxiliary heat simultaneously
defrost_operation.pressures_during_defrost: Momentarily match cooling mode since system reversed — do NOT diagnose charge during active defrost

coil_temp_termination_f.note: When outdoor coil sensor reads 57-70°F, frost is cleared, defrost terminates

after_defrost_delay_minutes.note: Normal delay before outdoor fan restarts

fault_vs_normal.normal: Defrost every 30-90 min, lasts 3-10 min, outdoor fan stops, steam/water visible from outdoor unit
fault_vs_normal.fault_stuck_in_defrost: System stays in defrost >15 min — check defrost board, defrost sensor/thermostat, reversing valve
fault_vs_normal.fault_never_defrosts: Outdoor coil fully iced over but no defrost — defrost board failed, sensor failed, or outdoor fan motor failed
fault_vs_normal.fault_defrosts_too_often: Every 10-15 min — low charge, outdoor fan fault, or dirty outdoor coil

heat_pump_defrost_cycle.houston_context: Houston rarely reaches defrost temperatures (<42°F OAT). Expect 0-5 defrost calls per winter season. When encountered, check if unit has been running in heating mode during a cold snap — not a common scenario.
heat_pump_defrost_cycle.national_context: Critical diagnostic in any climate where OAT regularly drops below 42°F (Northeast, Midwest, Pacific Northwest, mountains).

piston_bore_reference.description: Fixed orifice / piston bore sizes by tonnage. Used for Card #15 (piston mismatch diagnosis). Tech measures actual bore and compares to this table.
piston_bore_reference.measurement_note: Bore sizes referenced by last two digits (e.g. 0.068" = 'a 68'). Measure with orifice gauges. Bore that is 2+ sizes off from correct = mismatch.

r410a_bore_by_tonnage.note: 1.5T R-410A — same bore as some 2T units
2T R-410A. Some systems use 0.049 — verify against unit data plate.

piston_bore_reference.critical_warning: R-22 pistons are LARGER than R-410A pistons. Installing an R-22 piston in an R-410A system = severe overcharge risk. Installing R-410A piston in R-22 system = severe undercharge. Always verify refrigerant type before cross-referencing bore size.
piston_bore_reference.mismatch_symptoms: Too large = high suction, low superheat, flooding. Too small = low suction, high superheat, starved coil.

gas_furnace.description: Operating specifications for residential gas furnaces. Used in Tab G (Not Heating → Gas Furnace path).

natural_gas.note: Standard natural gas manifold pressure. Check with manometer at manifold test port with burners firing. Always verify against unit data plate — some manufacturers specify 3.8-4.0".

propane_lp.note: Propane requires higher pressure due to higher energy density. Propane manifold pressure MUST be set by qualified tech — incorrect pressure causes incomplete combustion or flame rollout.

incoming_supply_pressure.note: Measure at inlet to gas valve with burners firing. Below 5" WC = undersupply from meter/regulator.

temperature_rise.measurement: Delta T between return air temp and supply air temp at plenum, measured after 5+ minutes of steady operation
temperature_rise.how_to_read_nameplate: Check furnace data plate for rated temperature rise range — typically shown as '40-70°F' or '35-65°F'. Always use nameplate range, not generic target.
temperature_rise.too_low_below_40f: Low airflow or oversized furnace. Check blower speed, filter, coil restriction.
temperature_rise.too_high_above_70f: High-limit switch risk. Check blower, filter, restricted ductwork. Cracked heat exchanger possible if persistent.

inducer_motor.note: Inducer (draft inducer/combustion blower) amp draw varies by furnace size. Measure at inducer motor leads. High amps = failing motor bearing. No amp draw but motor present = capacitor or winding failure.

flame_sensor.note: Measure with multimeter on microamp (µA) scale at flame sensor lead. Below 1µA = sensor dirty or failed. Clean with fine steel wool or light abrasive. If still below 1µA after cleaning, replace sensor.
flame_sensor.symptom_of_failure: Furnace lights, runs 5-10 seconds, then shuts off and retries (lockout after 3 attempts)

high_limit_switch.manual_reset_models: Some high-efficiency furnaces have manual reset limits — check for reset button on limit
high_limit_switch.note: If high limit is tripping repeatedly, find root cause (airflow restriction, dirty filter, failed blower) before resetting. Repeated tripping with no cause found → heat exchanger crack investigation

flue_gas_co.note: Measure CO in flue gas at heat exchanger outlet. Above 100 ppm air-free = inspect heat exchanger for cracks. Above 200 ppm = shut down furnace immediately.

common_fault_tree.no_heat_no_ignition: Check: thermostat call → control board LED code → gas valve → ignitor (HSI or spark) → flame sensor → pressure switch (induced draft) → limit switch
common_fault_tree.no_heat_ignites_then_shuts_off: Most likely: dirty flame sensor (<1µA). Secondary: flame sensor wiring, cracked heat exchanger, flue restriction
common_fault_tree.no_heat_blower_runs_no_heat: Gas valve not opening → check 24V to gas valve, gas supply pressure, pressure switch
common_fault_tree.heat_but_insufficient: Check temperature rise (target 40-70°F), filter, blower speed, duct restrictions

ecm_blower_speed_tap_guide.description: ECM (Electronically Commutated Motor) blower motor speed tap settings by tonnage. Used after blower motor replacement to set correct airflow.
ecm_blower_speed_tap_guide.standard_cfm_rule: 400 CFM per ton of cooling capacity for standard comfort cooling.
ecm_blower_speed_tap_guide.houston_dehumidification_rule: 350 CFM per ton recommended for Houston due to extreme humidity. Slower air = longer coil contact time = more moisture removal.

tap_settings.note: ECM motors have 5 speed taps (1=lowest, 5=highest). Taps are energized via 24V signal from air handler control board. Factory default is usually Tap 3 or 4 for cooling.
tap_settings.tap_selection_method: Check the air handler installation manual for the CFM table. Find the row matching your TESP (total external static pressure) and select the tap that delivers the target CFM.
tap_settings.typical_tap_to_cfm_map: Varies by motor manufacturer (Genteq/Regal Rexnord most common). Tap 1 ≈ 60-70% of max CFM. Tap 3 ≈ 80-85%. Tap 5 = 100%. Always verify against manufacturer data.

wrong_tap_symptoms.too_low: Low delta-T, high humidity complaints, suction pressure drops (restricted airflow), coil icing
wrong_tap_symptoms.too_high: High static noise, high delta-T, low humidity control, shortened equipment life

tap_settings.constant_torque_vs_constant_cfm: Constant-torque ECM (older): tap = torque setpoint, CFM varies with static pressure. Constant-CFM/variable-speed ECM (newer): communicates with control board, auto-adjusts to maintain target CFM — tap selection handled by board programming.

ecm_blower_speed_tap_guide.heating_tap: For gas furnace or heat strip heating, a separate tap or higher speed is typically used. Check installation manual — heating CFM target is often 10-15% lower than cooling.

dual_fuel_detection.description: Dual fuel = heat pump outdoor unit paired with gas furnace air handler. System uses heat pump for efficiency above a switchover temp; gas furnace below that temp.
how_to_identify_in_field: Outdoor unit is a heat pump (has reversing valve, 4-way valve solenoid wire)
Indoor unit is a gas furnace (not air handler — has burner, heat exchanger, flue pipe)
Thermostat is a dual-fuel capable model (Honeywell Prestige IAQ, Ecobee Premium, Carrier Cor, Lennox iComfort S30)
Look for 'Hybrid Heat' or 'Dual Fuel' label on outdoor unit nameplate or marketing badge

switchover_temp_typical_f.houston_note: In Houston, dual-fuel switchover is rarely triggered — heat pump handles nearly all heating days. Switchover <40°F is typical but may never activate in a mild winter.

dual_fuel_detection.diagnostic_note: If a dual fuel system is 'not heating' in cold weather, first determine which system is active. Below switchover temp = gas furnace diagnostic. Above switchover temp = heat pump heating diagnostic.
dual_fuel_detection.common_fault_dual_fuel_specific: Thermostat not switching between modes — check switchover setpoint, verify both systems power up correctly, check O/B reversing valve wire.

condenser_fan_motor_diagnostic.description: Condenser fan motor failure diagnostic — separate from Card #4 (indoor blower). Condenser fan motor is one of the most common Houston repairs due to extreme heat.
condenser_fan_motor_diagnostic.fault_card_note: Condenser fan motor failure routes to its OWN diagnostic path, NOT Card #4 (which is indoor blower/air handler). These are completely separate components.
symptoms: Outdoor unit running but outdoor fan NOT spinning
High discharge pressure (no heat rejection from condenser)
Unit short-cycling on high pressure cutout
Buzzing sound from outdoor unit (capacitor weak, motor trying to start)
diagnostic_steps: 1. Confirm outdoor fan is not spinning (visual or listen for blade noise)
2. Check dual run capacitor fan side — weak cap is #1 cause of fan motor failure in Houston heat
3. Measure fan motor amps (see condenser_fan_motor_fla in electrical_specs)
4. Check for 24V to contactor and contactor closed — if closed and compressor runs but fan doesn't, motor or cap
5. Spin fan blade manually with power OFF — if stiff/doesn't spin freely = bearing failure, replace motor
condenser_fan_motor_diagnostic.houston_context: Houston's 100°F+ summers run outdoor fan motors at maximum duty cycle 8+ months/year. Fan motor MTBF in Houston is 7-10 years vs 12-15 years in moderate climates. Capacitor is usually first to fail (replace both cap and motor together when found).

parts_catalog.name: Run Capacitor (Single) — Compressor or Fan
parts_catalog.description: Single round run capacitor. Rated 370V or 440V. Common values: 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 55, 60 MFD.

parts_catalog.name: Dual Run Capacitor (Compressor + Fan)
parts_catalog.description: Oval dual-run capacitor. Replaces both compressor and fan motor caps in one unit. Most common residential part.

parts_catalog.name: Contactor (2-pole 30–40A)
parts_catalog.description: Standard 2-pole contactor for residential AC outdoor unit. 24V coil, 30A or 40A contacts.
parts_catalog.common_specs: 24V coil, 2-pole, 30A/40A contacts, Class P or A

parts_catalog.name: Contactor (3-pole 30-40A)
parts_catalog.description: 3-pole contactor for 3-phase residential/commercial-adjacent units. 24V coil, 30A or 40A contacts. Found on some 5-ton units, commercial-grade residential, and 3-phase applications.
parts_catalog.note: Do not substitute 2-pole for 3-pole application — leaves one leg uncontrolled. Rare in residential; common in light commercial and some 5T residential units.

parts_catalog.name: Contactor + Dual Run Capacitor (Combo Job)
parts_catalog.description: Most common combination job — tech often replaces both at same visit.

parts_catalog.name: Condenser Fan Motor (Outdoor Unit)
parts_catalog.description: OEM or aftermarket condenser fan motor. Typical specs: 1/4 HP, 825 RPM, 208/230V single-phase.
parts_catalog.common_specs: 1/5 to 1/3 HP, 825–1050 RPM, 208/230V, CW or CCW rotation

parts_catalog.name: Blower Motor — PSC (Permanent Split Capacitor)
parts_catalog.description: Standard blower motor for air handler. Multiple speed taps (L/M/H). Common in pre-2020 units.

parts_catalog.name: Blower Motor — ECM (Electronically Commutated Motor)
parts_catalog.description: Variable-speed ECM motor. Found in higher-efficiency units (2016+). Must match control module.
parts_catalog.common_specs: 1/2 to 1 HP, 208/230V, ECM 2.3 or ECM X13 type

parts_catalog.name: Condensate Drain Flush (Chemical + Labor)
parts_catalog.description: Chemical flush of condensate drain line. Includes flush tablet/treatment left in pan.

parts_catalog.name: Drain Pan Cleaning + Flush
parts_catalog.description: Full drain pan clean-out with algaecide treatment. Includes flush line.

parts_catalog.name: Condensate Pan Treatment Tablet
parts_catalog.description: Slow-release pan tablet. Prevents algae regrowth. Added at end of every drain service call.

parts_catalog.name: Air Filter — 1-inch Standard (MERV 8)
parts_catalog.description: Standard 1-inch pleated filter. MERV 8. Most common Houston residential size.

parts_catalog.name: Air Filter — 4-5-inch Media (MERV 11–13)
parts_catalog.description: High-capacity media filter. Lasts 6–12 months. Requires special media cabinet.

parts_catalog.name: Condenser Coil Cleaning (Outdoor)
parts_catalog.description: Chemical coil cleaner + rinse. Removes dirt, grass clippings, fin blockage. Top Houston upsell on service calls.

parts_catalog.name: Evaporator Coil Cleaning (Indoor/Air Handler)
parts_catalog.description: No-rinse evaporator coil cleaner spray. More labor due to access inside air handler.

parts_catalog.name: Refrigerant R-410A (per pound, add-on)
parts_catalog.description: R-410A Puron refrigerant. Price shown is retail installed per pound added. Includes recovery/handling fee.

parts_catalog.name: Refrigerant R-22 (per pound, reclaimed only)
parts_catalog.description: R-22 Freon. Reclaimed only since 2020 US ban. Very expensive and scarce. Recommend system replacement if R-22 unit needs significant recharge.
parts_catalog.important_note: If R-22 system needs more than 2 lbs, advise homeowner to consider full replacement. Repair vs replace math usually favors replace.

parts_catalog.name: Refrigerant Recovery + Evacuation + Full Recharge
parts_catalog.description: Full system recovery, evacuation (300 microns), and recharge to factory spec. Required after any refrigerant-side repair.
parts_catalog.note: Does not include cost of refrigerant itself (add per-lb cost above).

parts_catalog.name: Refrigerant Leak Search (Electronic + UV Dye)
parts_catalog.description: Electronic leak detector + UV dye injection to find leak source. Labor only — refrigerant billed separately.

parts_catalog.name: UV Dye Injection
parts_catalog.description: UV tracer dye injected into refrigerant circuit. Leak glows under UV light. Evidence photo value for homeowner PDF.

parts_catalog.name: TXV Valve (Thermostatic Expansion Valve)
parts_catalog.description: TXV metering device at indoor coil. Must match tonnage and refrigerant. Requires refrigerant recovery/recharge.

total_installed_houston.note: Includes recovery, replacement, evacuation, recharge labor. Does not include refrigerant cost.

parts_catalog.name: Fixed Orifice / Piston Metering Device
parts_catalog.description: Fixed orifice (piston) metering device. Bore size determines refrigerant flow. Must match tonnage exactly.

total_installed_houston.note: Includes recovery, replacement, evacuation, recharge labor.

parts_catalog.name: Reversing Valve (Heat Pump)
parts_catalog.description: 4-way reversing valve — heat pump only. Controls heat/cool mode switch. Requires full refrigerant handling.

parts_catalog.name: Control Board (Outdoor or Indoor Unit)
parts_catalog.description: Main control/defrost board. Brand-specific — must match model series. Outdoor or indoor board billed separately.

parts_catalog.name: Thermostat (Programmable / Smart)
parts_catalog.description: Replacement thermostat. Basic programmable ($50–100) to smart WiFi ($150–350).

parts_catalog.name: Hot Surface Ignitor (Gas Furnace)
parts_catalog.description: Silicon carbide or silicon nitride ignitor. Specific to furnace model. Very fragile — handle with care.

parts_catalog.name: Flame Sensor Rod (Gas Furnace)
parts_catalog.description: Stainless steel sensing rod. Often cleanable with steel wool before replacement. Very low cost part.

parts_catalog.name: Evaporator Coil (2–3 Ton)
parts_catalog.description: Replacement A-coil or slab coil for air handler. Must match system tonnage, refrigerant, and TXV/piston type.

parts_catalog.name: Evaporator Coil (4–5 Ton)
parts_catalog.description: Large evaporator coil. Requires more labor and refrigerant for recharge.

parts_catalog.name: Condenser Coil (Outdoor Unit)
parts_catalog.description: Replacement condenser coil. OEM or aftermarket. Requires refrigerant recovery/recharge.

parts_catalog.name: Compressor Replacement — 1.5 Ton
parts_catalog.note: 1.5T compressors common in Houston condos, townhomes, small additions, and single-room additions. If under warranty, labor only: $380-600. If R-22 system, add $250-400 for refrigerant.

parts_catalog.name: Compressor Replacement — 2 Ton
parts_catalog.note: If under warranty, labor only: $450–700. If R-22 system, add $300–500 for refrigerant.

parts_catalog.name: Ductwork Repair (accessible section)
parts_catalog.description: Seal and repair accessible duct section. Mastic sealant + foil tape. Attic ductwork is typical.

labor_hours.note: Attic access in Houston summer adds significant labor time.

parts_catalog.name: Wiring / Terminal Repair
parts_catalog.description: Repair or replace damaged low-voltage or line-voltage wiring. Terminal replacement, heat-shrink splices.

parts_catalog.name: Terminal Cleaning + Retorque (Card #16)
parts_catalog.description: Clean oxidized/loose terminals, retorque to spec, verify voltage drop. No parts in most cases.

parts_catalog.name: Service Call / Diagnostic Fee
parts_catalog.description: Standard diagnostic fee. Many Houston companies waive if repair is booked.

parts_catalog.name: Annual Service / Tune-Up
parts_catalog.description: Annual preventive maintenance. Base labor + drain flush tablet. Findings add as separate line items.

labor_rates_houston.flat_rate_note: Most Houston companies use flat-rate pricing. Hourly rates apply mainly for custom or unusual jobs.

attic_work_premium.note: Houston attic temps reach 140–160°F in summer. Attic labor gets premium pricing.

r22_handling_surcharge.note: Additional fee for R-22 certification and reclaim handling

fault_card_estimates.tech_notes: Most common summer call in Houston. Always check contactor while panel is open. Recommend combo replacement if contactor shows any pitting.
Fastest possible resolution. Photo confirms diagnosis instantly. Always add drain flush tablet if system has been running with a clogged filter — condensate backup likely.
Pitting on contact face = definitive failure. Voltage reading confirms. Always check capacitor simultaneously.
ECM motors require exact replacement — cannot substitute generic. PSC motors more flexible. Check blower wheel for debris while motor is out.
#1 Houston call. High humidity, algae grows fast. Always leave pan tablet. Recommend quarterly flush in Houston climate.
Voltage drop reading confirms. Photo of terminal strip shows burn marks for homeowner PDF.
Error code routes directly to fault. Photo of LED board is the input. Requires Task #12 (brand error code DB) for full auto-routing. Until then, Gemini interprets.
XGBoost on pressure pattern confirms leak. UV dye photo is the homeowner PDF evidence. Always find leak source — do not just recharge and leave.
Let coil thaw first (1–3 hrs). Root cause is almost always dirty filter or low charge. Fix root cause, not just symptom.
Most expensive repair. If unit is 8+ years old, advise replacement vs repair. RLA reading over nameplate = definitive over-amping evidence for homeowner PDF.
Photo of cracked ignitor or oxidized sensor = compelling homeowner evidence. Micro-amp reading confirms flame sensor fault.
Readings in heat mode determine stuck direction. Rarely fails in Houston (low heat demand). Most failure is stuck in cooling mode.
Houston attic work is extreme in summer. Scope attic early morning only. Bill attic premium. Mastic + foil tape is correct fix — duct tape is NOT an approved sealant.
YOLO strongest use case — dirty coil is visible from outside unit. XGBoost on high discharge pressure confirms. Good upsell on service calls.
Bore measurement vs nameplate tonnage confirms mismatch. Common after equipment swaps where matching piston was not installed.
Voltage drop >3V confirms. Thermal camera fastest. Point thermometer + voltage drop = 85–90% catch rate without camera. Clean + retorque — no part usually needed.
High discharge + high subcooling = overcharge. Recover excess refrigerant to factory weight. XGBoost distinguishes from dirty coil via subcooling level.

total_estimate_houston.note: No repair estimate — this leads to system replacement recommendation

fault_card_estimates.tech_notes: Gemini multi-factor reasoning. Load calculation required. Square footage + insulation + windows + Houston climate factor. Estimate is for new system (out of scope for repair).

Green/white powder deposits on evaporator coil face = formicary corrosion. Caused by formic acid from VOCs in home. Usually leads to coil pinhole leak. Coil replacement required. UV dye confirms active leak.

climate_facts.hurricane_season_note: June–November. Power surges after storms spike capacitor and contactor failures dramatically.

top_seasonal_fault_patterns.notes: Peak demand. High heat = capacitor stress. High humidity = drain clogs weekly.
Pre-season tune-ups spike. First hot day of year reveals failures that sat over winter.
Gas furnace season. Houston winters are mild but first cold snap causes surge in furnace calls.
Power surges after Houston storms (Beryl, Harvey, etc.) spike electrical component failures across the city simultaneously.

most_stocked_parts_houston_truck: Condenser fan motors: 1/4 HP, 1/6 HP universal

error_code_db.schema_note: Error codes split by brand family. 'led_flash' = non-communicating single-LED systems. 'communicating' = digital thermostat codes. 'mini_split' = display codes on indoor unit. All codes verified from manufacturer service manuals and tech bulletins.
error_code_db.how_to_use: When tech sees an error code or LED flash pattern: 1) Identify brand family. 2) Match to code type (led_flash vs communicating vs mini_split). 3) Use diagnosis field to route to correct decision tree card.

led_flash_system.description: Single LED on air handler/indoor control board. Non-communicating standard split systems. Count flashes before the pause.

codes.meaning: Normal standby — 24V power present, no call for heat/cool
codes.action: Normal — monitor

codes.meaning: No power to control board
codes.action: Check 24V transformer, fuses, disconnect

codes.meaning: Reversed line voltage polarity or control board failure
codes.action: Swap L1/L2 at disconnect; if persists, replace board

codes.meaning: System lockout — failed to ignite after 3 attempts (auto-resets in ~1 hr)
codes.action: Check gas valve, ignitor, flame sensor

codes.meaning: Pressure switch stuck closed (before inducer starts)
codes.action: Check for blocked condensate tubing, check pressure switch

codes.meaning: Draft pressure switch stuck open (inducer running but switch not closing)
codes.action: Check inducer motor, vent blockage, pressure switch hose

codes.meaning: Open high limit switch — overheating
codes.action: Check filter, airflow, blower motor; reset after cooling

codes.meaning: Flame sensed without call for heat — gas valve leaking by
codes.action: Replace gas valve

codes.meaning: Rollout switch open (flame rollout) or reversed polarity
codes.action: Check heat exchanger for cracks; check gas pressure

codes.meaning: Low flame signal — dirty or cracked flame sensor
codes.action: Clean flame sensor with fine steel wool

codes.meaning: Ignitor fault — open element or no 120V to ignitor circuit
codes.action: Measure ignitor resistance (40–90 Ω hot surface); replace if open

codes.meaning: Reversed inducer / heat pump AUX lockout (model dependent)
codes.action: Check wiring polarity; check AUX heat sequencer

communicating_infinity.description: Carrier Infinity / Bryant Evolution variable-speed systems. Codes shown on thermostat display. Uses 3-digit numeric codes.

codes.meaning: Low pressure switch open — low refrigerant or low load
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check metering device

codes.meaning: High pressure switch open — blocked condenser, overcharge, fan failure
codes.action: Check condenser coil, fan motor, charge

codes.meaning: Loss of charge protection — system pressure too low
codes.action: Leak check; evacuate and recharge

codes.meaning: Outdoor unit communication error
codes.action: Check communication wiring between indoor and outdoor units

codes.meaning: High discharge temperature lockout
codes.action: Check charge, condenser airflow, compressor efficiency

codes.meaning: High ambient temp safety — OAT too high for operation
codes.action: Check condenser fan; verify not recirculating hot air

codes.meaning: Compressor current fault — high amperage draw
codes.action: Check capacitor, check compressor windings, check suction pressure

codes.meaning: Compressor trip / locked rotor
codes.action: Hard start kit if first offense; compressor replacement if repeat

codes.meaning: Indoor unit communication failure
codes.action: Check communication wiring, check air handler board

codes.meaning: Thermostat communication failure
codes.action: Check thermostat wiring, replace Infinity thermostat if needed

led_flash_system.description: Single LED on indoor control board (non-communicating XR/XB models). Count flashes before 3-second pause.

codes.meaning: System lockout — failed heat/cool sequence
codes.action: Power cycle; check refrigerant, gas supply, ignitor

codes.meaning: Vent pressure switch stuck open
codes.action: Check vent blockage, inducer motor, pressure switch tubing

codes.meaning: Open high limit switch — overheating
codes.action: Check filter, blower motor, supply/return airflow

codes.meaning: Flame sensed without call for heat
codes.action: Replace gas valve

codes.meaning: Line voltage polarity reversed
codes.action: Swap L1/L2 at main disconnect

codes.meaning: Gas valve circuit open — no 24V signal reaching valve
codes.action: Check gas valve wiring, check board output on W terminal

codes.meaning: Low flame sense signal — dirty flame sensor
codes.action: Clean flame sensor rod; check microamp draw (should be 1.5+ µA)

codes.meaning: Ignitor circuit fault — open hot surface ignitor
codes.action: Measure ignitor resistance; replace if >120 Ω or open

communicating_xl_comfortlink.description: Trane XL / American Standard Platinum with ComfortLink II thermostat. Numeric codes on thermostat.

codes.meaning: High pressure lockout
codes.action: Check condenser coil cleanliness, fan motor, refrigerant charge

codes.meaning: Low pressure lockout
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge, TXV/piston, check for low load condition

codes.meaning: Loss of charge — pressure critically low
codes.action: Leak check entire system; evacuate and recharge

codes.meaning: High discharge temperature
codes.action: Check charge, condenser airflow, compressor valve efficiency

codes.meaning: Compressor monitor trip — current or winding protection
codes.action: Check capacitor first; check compressor amp draw vs RLA

codes.meaning: Indoor coil temperature sensor fault — open or shorted
codes.action: Replace thermistor sensor on indoor coil

codes.meaning: Outdoor ambient temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Replace OAT thermistor on outdoor unit

codes.meaning: High pressure alert — nearing trip point
codes.action: Check condenser coil, head pressure rising

codes.meaning: Compressor lockout — repeat high amps or trip events
codes.action: Compressor likely failing; hard start kit or replacement

codes.meaning: Blower module communication failure
codes.action: Check ECM blower wiring; replace blower control module if needed

led_flash_system.description: Single LED on air handler/furnace control board. Non-communicating systems. Note: 2 flashes/sec during normal heating = normal operation, NOT an error.

codes.meaning: Normal standby — 24V power present, no call
codes.action: Normal

codes.meaning: Normal operation — furnace actively heating
codes.action: Normal — not an error code

codes.meaning: Reversed line voltage polarity or control board fault
codes.action: Swap L1/L2; replace board if persists

codes.meaning: Furnace lockout — 3 failed ignition attempts. Auto-resets after ~1 hour.
codes.action: Check gas supply, ignitor, flame sensor, gas valve

codes.meaning: Pressure switch stuck closed (fault before inducer starts)
codes.action: Check condensate trap/drain, check pressure switch port for blockage

codes.meaning: Draft pressure switch stuck open (inducer ON but switch not closing)
codes.action: Check vent blockage, inducer wheel, pressure switch and tubing

codes.meaning: Open limit circuit — overheating protection tripped
codes.action: Replace filter, check airflow, check blower motor capacitor

codes.meaning: Flame sensed without heat call — gas valve passing when off
codes.action: Replace gas valve; do not operate unit

codes.meaning: Rollout switch open OR low flame signal (board-dependent)
codes.action: Check heat exchanger for cracks; check gas manifold pressure

codes.meaning: Low flame sense signal — flame sensor dirty or cracked
codes.action: Clean flame sensor with fine steel wool; check µA reading

codes.meaning: Inducer feedback error / control board fault
codes.action: Check inducer motor wiring; replace control board if persists

codes.meaning: Control board / checksum fault
codes.action: Replace control board

communicating_comfortnet.description: Goodman/Amana GSXC/DSXC/AVPVC systems with ComfortNet (CTK) communicating thermostat. Alpha-numeric codes.

codes.meaning: Indoor unit communication failure — no signal from air handler
codes.action: Check communication wiring; check air handler board

codes.meaning: Outdoor unit communication failure
codes.action: Check communication wiring to condenser; check outdoor board

codes.meaning: Thermostat communication failure
codes.action: Replace CTK thermostat

codes.meaning: High pressure lockout — outdoor high pressure switch tripped
codes.action: Check condenser coil, condenser fan motor, refrigerant charge

codes.meaning: Low pressure lockout — outdoor low pressure switch tripped
codes.action: Check charge; check for low load condition or TXV restriction

codes.meaning: High discharge temperature — compressor discharge too hot
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge, head pressure, condenser airflow

codes.meaning: Loss of charge protection — critically low suction pressure
codes.action: Leak check; do not operate; evacuate and recharge

codes.meaning: Compressor overload / high current draw
codes.action: Check capacitor, check amp draw vs RLA nameplate

led_flash_system.description: Single LED on air handler/furnace control board. Standard non-communicating split systems. Count flashes before pause.

codes.meaning: Equipment lockout — multiple faults triggered safety shutdown
codes.action: Power cycle; investigate underlying fault code history

codes.meaning: Low pressure switch open — low refrigerant or low load
codes.action: Check charge; check TXV/piston; check outdoor ambient temp

codes.meaning: High pressure switch open — blocked coil, overcharge, fan failure
codes.action: Check condenser coil, condenser fan motor, refrigerant level

codes.meaning: Condensate drain pan full — float switch tripped
codes.action: Clear drain; flush with nitrogen; replace float switch if faulty

codes.meaning: High supply air temperature — overheating in supply plenum
codes.action: Check filter, duct restrictions, blower operation

codes.meaning: Low supply air temperature — coil icing
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge, airflow, filter; look for ice on coil

codes.meaning: Gas valve fault / modulating valve fault
codes.action: Check 24V to gas valve on W terminal; replace valve if no output

codes.meaning: Ignitor fault — failed ignition sequence
codes.action: Measure ignitor resistance; check 120V supply to ignitor

codes.meaning: Communication fault between boards (model-specific)
codes.action: Check wiring between indoor and outdoor boards

communicating_econet.description: Rheem Prestige (RP20/RP17) and Ruud Ultra (UP20/UP17) with EcoNet communicating controls. 2-digit numeric codes.

codes.meaning: Standby / normal operation
codes.action: Normal

codes.meaning: Cooling mode active — no fault
codes.action: Normal

codes.meaning: High line voltage fault — line voltage over 253V
codes.action: Check utility voltage at disconnect; contact utility if persistent

codes.meaning: Low line voltage fault — power brown-out condition
codes.action: Check voltage at disconnect; check wire gauge from panel

codes.meaning: On-board fuse open — low voltage wiring fault
codes.action: Check for shorted low voltage wiring at R and C terminals

codes.meaning: Indoor unit communication failure
codes.action: Check communication wiring; check air handler control board

codes.meaning: Thermostat communication failure — no signal from EcoNet control
codes.action: Check EcoNet thermostat wiring and programming

codes.meaning: High pressure switch fault — outdoor high pressure tripped
codes.action: Check condenser coil, fan motor, ambient temp, refrigerant charge

codes.meaning: Low pressure switch fault — outdoor low pressure tripped
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check metering device

codes.meaning: High discharge temperature — compressor head too hot
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge, condenser airflow, valve efficiency

codes.meaning: Loss of charge — pressure critically low; system locked out
codes.action: Leak check entire system; evacuate and recharge

codes.meaning: Compressor lockout — repeat high pressure or current events
codes.action: Evaluate compressor for replacement; hard start kit interim

codes.meaning: Compressor overcurrent — amp draw above protection limit
codes.action: Check capacitor; measure compressor amps vs RLA nameplate

codes.meaning: Minimum airflow not met — static pressure too high
codes.action: Check filter, duct sizing, blower speed setting, coil cleanliness

led_flash_system.description: York/Heil/Coleman (Johnson Controls) non-communicating systems. YorkGuard VI uses two LEDs; older boards use single LED. Both count flash sequences.

codes.meaning: Indoor control board EEPROM error — memory fault
codes.action: Replace indoor control board

codes.meaning: Indoor/outdoor communication malfunction — wiring or board failure
codes.action: Check communication wiring; check both boards

codes.meaning: Indoor fan speed malfunction — ECM motor or control
codes.action: Check ECM motor wiring; replace ECM module or motor

codes.meaning: Indoor room temperature sensor error — open or shorted
codes.action: Replace room temperature thermistor

codes.meaning: Evaporator coil temperature sensor error
codes.action: Replace evaporator coil thermistor

codes.meaning: Refrigerant leak detection system malfunction
codes.action: Check leak detector sensor; check charge

codes.meaning: Water level alarm malfunction — condensate overflow or sensor fault
codes.action: Clear drain line; check float switch; replace if faulty

codes.meaning: Overload protection activation — compressor or motor overload
codes.action: Check amp draws; allow cool-down; check capacitor

codes.meaning: Outdoor ambient temperature sensor error
codes.action: Replace outdoor OAT thermistor

codes.meaning: IPM to outdoor PCB communication fault
codes.action: Check inverter board wiring; replace IPM if persists

codes.meaning: Discharge temperature overheat protection
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge, condenser airflow, compressor efficiency

codes.meaning: Outdoor DC fan motor fault — inverter-driven fan failure
codes.action: Check fan motor winding resistance; replace outdoor fan motor

codes.meaning: Power voltage too high or too low
codes.action: Check line voltage at disconnect; check utility voltage

codes.meaning: Defrost temperature sensor failure — heat pump models
codes.action: Replace defrost thermistor on outdoor coil

codes.meaning: Communicating system: control board fault
codes.action: Check all wiring; replace outdoor control board if persists

led_flash_system.description: Single LED on furnace/air handler control board (non-communicating Dave Lennox Signature and Merit series). Count flashes.

codes.meaning: Normal standby — 24V powered, waiting for call
codes.action: Normal

codes.meaning: Ignition lockout — 3 failed attempts, system locked out
codes.action: Check gas supply, ignitor, flame sensor, gas valve

codes.meaning: Pressure switch fault — stuck open or closed at wrong time
codes.action: Check vent, inducer motor, condensate, pressure switch tubing

codes.meaning: High limit switch tripped — overheating
codes.action: Check filter, airflow, blower motor capacitor, supply plenum

codes.meaning: Open rollout switch — flame rollout detected
codes.action: STOP: Do not reset without inspecting heat exchanger for cracks

codes.meaning: Reversed polarity at line voltage supply
codes.action: Swap L1/L2 at furnace disconnect

codes.meaning: Flame sensed without call for heat — gas valve leaking
codes.action: Replace gas valve immediately

codes.meaning: Control board fault / equipment lockout
codes.action: Replace control board

codes.meaning: Low flame sense signal — dirty or failed flame sensor
codes.action: Clean flame sensor; measure µA (should be >1.5 µA)

communicating_icomfort.description: Lennox iComfort thermostat with Dave Lennox Signature XC21/XC20/SL28 communicating systems. Alpha-numeric codes on iComfort display.

codes.meaning: Control hardware error — outdoor unit board fault
codes.action: Reset system; replace outdoor control board if recurs

codes.meaning: Blower module communication failure — ECM blower not responding
codes.action: Check blower module wiring; replace ECM module or motor

codes.meaning: Reduced airflow alert — filter dirty or airflow restriction
codes.action: Replace filter; check coil cleanliness; check duct static pressure

codes.meaning: Compressor start failure — cannot reach target RPM (variable-speed)
codes.action: Check capacitor (if applicable), check compressor windings, check voltage

codes.meaning: Communication fault — loss of signal between thermostat and system
codes.action: Check 4-wire communication cable; check terminal connections

codes.meaning: Indoor air temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Replace indoor air thermistor

codes.meaning: Outdoor air temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Replace outdoor OAT thermistor

codes.meaning: High pressure lockout
codes.action: Check condenser coil, fan motor, ambient temp, charge

codes.meaning: Low pressure lockout
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check for low load conditions

codes.meaning: High discharge temperature — discharge line too hot
codes.action: Check charge, condenser airflow, compressor valve efficiency

mini_split_display.description: Mitsubishi ductless mini-split indoor unit display codes. Shown on indoor unit LED or remote controller. Most common codes for residential P, MSZ, MXZ series.
mini_split_display.notes: U4 (communication fault) is the #1 most common code seen in the field — accounts for ~40% of all Mitsubishi service calls.

codes.meaning: Power supply problem — voltage issue at outdoor unit
codes.action: Check line voltage at outdoor disconnect; check wire size

codes.meaning: Indoor/outdoor communication error — serial signal loss
codes.action: Check 2-wire communication cable between indoor and outdoor units

codes.meaning: Indoor unit fan motor locked / fault
codes.action: Check indoor fan motor winding; check motor capacitor if belt type

codes.meaning: Indoor air temperature sensor fault — open or short
codes.action: Replace indoor air thermistor (usually behind front panel)

codes.meaning: Indoor coil temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Replace indoor coil thermistor

codes.meaning: Communication error between indoor PCB and display — most common board fault
codes.action: Check wiring between indoor PCB and display; replace PCB if needed

codes.meaning: Outdoor unit fan motor fault — DC fan failure
codes.action: Check outdoor fan motor resistance; check inverter board output

codes.meaning: AC current protection activation — high current draw at startup
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check compressor windings

codes.meaning: Drain pump fault — condensate drain not pumping
codes.action: Check drain pump; clear drain line obstruction; replace pump if failed

codes.meaning: Overvoltage or undervoltage protection
codes.action: Check line voltage; check for brown-out condition

codes.meaning: Overcurrent protection — compressor drawing too many amps
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check compressor amp draw vs nameplate

codes.meaning: Discharge pipe temperature sensor fault (or discharge temp too high)
codes.action: If sensor: replace discharge thermistor. If temp: check charge, condenser

codes.meaning: Drain level sensor fault — float switch triggered
codes.action: Clear drain line; check float switch; confirm drain pump works

codes.meaning: Compressor lockout — consecutive protection trips
codes.action: Check charge, voltage, compressor efficiency; hard start kit or replacement

codes.meaning: Indoor heat exchanger temperature fault — coil too hot or too cold
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check indoor coil for ice or dirt

codes.meaning: Compressor temperature sensor fault or overtemp
codes.action: Check discharge thermistor; check refrigerant charge

codes.meaning: Reverse phase or open phase detected (3-phase models)
codes.action: Check phase rotation and all 3 phases present at disconnect

codes.meaning: Power supply abnormality — voltage drop or spike at outdoor PCB
codes.action: Check line voltage quality; check capacitors on outdoor PCB

codes.meaning: Communication failure — outdoor unit to indoor unit
codes.action: Check 2-wire comm cable; check both PCBs for damage

codes.meaning: Communication failure — indoor unit to outdoor unit (MOST COMMON). Usually caused by wiring issue or power loss to outdoor unit.
codes.action: 1) Check outdoor disconnect on. 2) Check comm wire connections. 3) Verify 208/230V at outdoor unit. 4) Replace outdoor PCB if all OK.

codes.meaning: Communication fault between multiple indoor units (multi-zone systems)
codes.action: Check wiring between all indoor units and outdoor unit

codes.meaning: Outdoor unit fan motor fault — DC inverter fan not running
codes.action: Check outdoor fan motor; check inverter module output for fan circuit

mini_split_display.description: Daikin standalone systems (not Goodman/Amana rebadged). Ductless mini-splits and Aurora heat pumps. Display on indoor unit.

codes.meaning: Indoor PCB fault
codes.action: Replace indoor PCB

codes.meaning: Drain system fault — condensate not draining
codes.action: Clear drain; check float switch; check drain pump

codes.meaning: Freeze protection / high heat protection — coil temp extreme
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check airflow; check filters

codes.meaning: Indoor fan motor fault
codes.action: Check fan motor winding; replace motor or PCB

codes.meaning: Indoor coil temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Replace indoor coil thermistor

codes.meaning: Indoor air temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Replace indoor air thermistor

codes.meaning: Outdoor PCB fault
codes.action: Replace outdoor PCB / inverter board

codes.meaning: High pressure switch protection — head pressure too high
codes.action: Check condenser coil, fan motor, refrigerant charge

codes.meaning: Low pressure switch protection — suction pressure too low
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check metering device

codes.meaning: Inverter overcurrent protection — compressor drawing high current
codes.action: Check charge; check compressor windings; check inverter board

codes.meaning: Compressor motor fault — locked or open winding
codes.action: Check compressor windings and megohm resistance; replace compressor

codes.meaning: Outdoor fan motor fault
codes.action: Check outdoor DC fan motor; check inverter module

codes.meaning: Electronic expansion valve fault
codes.action: Replace EEV; check for contamination in refrigerant circuit

codes.meaning: Discharge pipe temperature too high — compressor protection
codes.action: Check refrigerant charge; check condenser airflow

codes.meaning: Position sensor fault — DC compressor startup issue
codes.action: Check compressor wiring; replace outdoor PCB/inverter

codes.meaning: Discharge temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Replace discharge thermistor

codes.meaning: Electrical box high temperature protection
codes.action: Check ventilation around electrical box; check inverter board cooling

codes.meaning: Low refrigerant charge detected
codes.action: Leak check; evacuate and recharge

codes.meaning: Low voltage protection — supply voltage drop
codes.action: Check line voltage; check wire gauge

codes.meaning: Communication fault between indoor and outdoor units
codes.action: Check comm cable; check both PCBs

routing_guide.description: When a tech reports an error code, use this guide to route to the correct decision tree card and service path.

rules.code_pattern: Any HIGH PRESSURE code (Carrier 176, Trane 101, Rheem 40, York E3/F4, Goodman E4, Lennox E6, Mitsubishi P4/P8, Daikin E3)
Any LOW PRESSURE / LOSS OF CHARGE code (Carrier 173/178, Trane 102/103, Rheem 41/44, Goodman E5/E8, Lennox E7, Mitsubishi P8, Daikin E4/U0)
always_check_first: refrigerant leak (leak detector or UV dye)

rules.code_pattern: Any COMMUNICATION code (Carrier 179/225/226, Trane 201, Rheem 34/35, York E1, Goodman E1/E2/E3, Lennox C2, Mitsubishi U3/U4, Daikin U4)
Any COMPRESSOR LOCKOUT / HIGH CURRENT code (Carrier 183/184, Trane 108/184, Rheem 45/46, Goodman E9, Mitsubishi P2/P6, Daikin E5/E6)
always_check_first: compressor amp draw vs nameplate RLA

rules.code_pattern: Any DRAIN / WATER code (Rheem 4_flash, York EE, Mitsubishi E9/P5, Daikin A3)
Any AIRFLOW / LIMIT code (Carrier 4_flash, Trane 4_flash, Rheem 5_flash/80, Goodman 4_flash, Lennox 3_flash, Lennox 312)
always_check_first: blower motor amp draw and capacitor

routing_guide.communication_fault: CH05 → Card #11 or communication wiring check
routing_guide.fan_fault: CH10, CH67 → condenser or blower fan diagnostic

codes.action: Test sensor resistance, replace if out of spec

codes.description: Indoor inlet pipe temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Check wiring and sensor resistance

codes.description: Indoor outlet pipe temperature sensor fault
codes.action: Check wiring and sensor resistance

codes.description: Communication error between indoor and outdoor unit
codes.likely_cause: Loose communication wire, PCB fault, power issue
codes.action: Check comm wiring (typically 2-conductor shielded), check both PCBs
Test sensor at outdoor board

codes.description: Indoor fan motor locked / DC motor fault
codes.likely_cause: Fan motor seized or PCB driver failure
codes.action: Check fan rotation, replace motor if seized; if free, replace PCB

codes.likely_cause: Dirty outdoor coil, overcharge, outdoor fan failure, high ambient
codes.action: Clean coil, check pressures, verify fan operation

codes.likely_cause: Compressor failing, refrigerant issue, high ambient
codes.action: Check compressor amps vs VFD nameplate, check pressures

codes.likely_cause: Sensor or wiring on outdoor PCB
codes.action: Check sensor and connector at outdoor board
Check outdoor fan rotation, motor resistance, replace motor or PCB

samsung.display_type: E-prefix error codes on indoor unit display
samsung.code_structure: E1xx = Indoor, E2xx = Outdoor, E3xx = Compressor, E4xx = Fan, E5xx = Protection, E6xx = Valve

codes.description: Indoor room thermistor short or open
codes.action: Replace indoor room temperature sensor
Replace outdoor temp sensor

codes.likely_cause: Fan motor or hall sensor failure
codes.action: Check motor rotation, replace motor or PCB

codes.likely_cause: Comm wire loose, PCB fault, power imbalance
codes.action: Check 2-wire comm cable, inspect both PCBs
Check pressures, clean coil, verify outdoor fan

codes.likely_cause: Low refrigerant, restriction, outdoor coil iced
codes.action: Check pressures and charge, look for ice on outdoor coil
Check compressor amps, discharge temp, refrigerant charge

codes.likely_cause: Sensor or wiring at outdoor PCB
codes.action: Replace sensor

gree.display_type: E/H/F prefix codes on indoor display

codes.likely_cause: Dirty outdoor coil, overcharge, outdoor fan failure
codes.action: Check high-side pressure, clean coil, verify fan

codes.likely_cause: Low charge, dirty filter/coil, or low airflow causing indoor coil to freeze
codes.action: Check suction pressure, filter, indoor airflow

codes.likely_cause: Low charge, dirty coils, high ambient, restricted airflow
codes.action: Check discharge temp (<185°F normal), check pressures and coils

codes.likely_cause: Compressor struggling, high load, inverter board fault
codes.action: Check compressor amps vs VFD nameplate

codes.likely_cause: Supply voltage below acceptable range (<187V for 208/230V)
codes.action: Check incoming voltage at disconnect
Check 2-wire comm, inspect PCBs

codes.likely_cause: Two zones calling for opposite modes simultaneously
codes.action: Check thermostat settings on all zones

codes.description: Refrigerant leak detection / low charge alarm
codes.action: Check suction pressure, perform leak search

codes.likely_cause: Compressor over-amping, bearing failure, locked rotor
codes.action: Measure compressor amps, check motor windings

codes.description: IPM (Intelligent Power Module) protection — outdoor inverter board
codes.likely_cause: Inverter module overheating, compressor short, DC bus fault
codes.action: Check outdoor PCB, compressor winding resistance, verify proper airflow to control box

pioneer.display_type: E/P/F prefix numeric codes on indoor display

routing_guide.fan: E0 or P4 → fan motor diagnostic

codes.action: Check outdoor PCB and compressor windings

codes.likely_cause: Dirty outdoor coil, overcharge, fan fault
codes.action: Check high-side pressure and outdoor coil

codes.description: Anti-freeze / indoor coil temp too low
codes.likely_cause: Low charge, low airflow, dirty filter
codes.action: Check suction pressure and airflow

codes.likely_cause: Low charge, dirty coil, restricted airflow
codes.action: Check discharge temp and pressures
Check compressor amps
Check communication cable
Replace sensor
Replace sensor
Replace sensor

codes.description: Outdoor unit discharge temperature protection / compressor inverter fault
codes.likely_cause: High discharge temp OR inverter module fault
codes.action: Check discharge temp, if normal check outdoor PCB
Check suction pressure, perform leak search
Clean filter and reset counter

codes.likely_cause: System running at capacity limit, high load condition
codes.action: Check for restricted airflow, high ambient conditions

mrcool.display_type: P/E prefix codes on indoor display or remote
mrcool.note: MrCool DIY series are popular in Houston for add-on rooms and garages. Designed for homeowner installation — tech may encounter non-standard lineset work.

codes.action: Check incoming voltage at disconnect

codes.likely_cause: Inverter board fault or compressor failure
codes.action: Check outdoor PCB, compressor winding resistance

codes.likely_cause: Low charge, dirty coils, high ambient
codes.action: Check discharge temp and pressures
Check suction pressure, perform leak search
Check 2-wire communication cable

codes.likely_cause: Low charge, low airflow, dirty filter
codes.action: Check suction pressure and indoor airflow

codes.likely_cause: Dirty outdoor coil, overcharge, fan fault
codes.action: Check high-side pressure, clean outdoor coil
Wait 3-10 min for defrost to complete. NOT a fault code.

bosch.display_type: E-prefix numeric codes on indoor display

codes.action: Replace indoor ambient sensor
Replace indoor coil sensor
Replace outdoor ambient sensor
Replace outdoor coil sensor
Replace discharge sensor
Check 2-wire comm cable and both PCBs
Check suction pressure; Bosch Climate uses R-32 — use R-32 PT chart

codes.likely_cause: Dirty outdoor coil, overcharge, fan fault
codes.action: Check high-side pressure with R-32 chart (higher than R-410A)

legacy_model_prefix_lookup.description: Pre-2010 model prefix reference for Step Zero model identification. When OCR reads a nameplate that doesn't match current model_prefixes in brands[], check here before failing the lookup.
legacy_model_prefix_lookup.fallback_guidance: If model prefix matches a legacy prefix, route to the parent brand's diagnostic tree. Assume R-22 refrigerant unless nameplate shows otherwise. Alert tech: 'This appears to be a pre-2010 unit. Confirm refrigerant type before connecting gauges.'

lifecycle_rules._comment: Board Session 8 §5 — age-based label switching and lifespan data. avg_lifespan_years drives remainingLife calc; label_sets drive Good/Better/Best tier copy.

age_buckets.label: Young System
age_buckets.description: System is within early life — repairs are cost-effective and extend system life.

age_buckets.label: Mid-Life System
age_buckets.description: System approaching mid-life — weigh repair cost vs. remaining useful life.

age_buckets.label: End-of-Life System
age_buckets.description: System near or past expected lifespan — replacement is the most economical long-term choice.

best.sub_label: New system while it's a good time

remaining_life_calc.note: Clamp to 0 if result is negative. unit_age_years = current_year - install_year.
